steps.title: Recognize the non-negativity of the quadratic term
steps.content: The function is $$g(x) = x^2 + 55$$. For any real number $x$, the term $x^2$ is always non-negative: $x^2 \geq 0$.

steps.title: Calculate the minimum value
steps.content:
The smallest possible value of $x^2$ is \$0$, occurring when $x = 0$. Therefore, the minimum value of $g(x)$ is:
$$g(0) = 0^2 + 55 = 55$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
